pi_items.source: Task description specifies creating a new style named 'cities_sorted' for the 'ne' workspace
Setup script setup_geoserver.sh line 137 references 'workspaces/ne' for creating PostGIS data store and publishing layers

Natural Earth 110m populated places shapefile contains POP_MAX field (confirmed by pyshp analysis). When imported to PostGIS via ogr2ogr, field names are lowercased to 'pop_max'. This is the maximum population estimate for each city.
Hex color code #FF0000 verified to be pure red (RGB: 255, 0, 0)
Setup script setup_geoserver.sh line 120 imports shapefile with -nln 'ne_populated_places'. Line 186 publishes this layer as 'ne_populated_places' in GeoServer.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the Natural Earth 110m Populated Places dataset, a real open-source geospatial dataset containing approximately 7,000 city/town points with attributes including POP_MAX (maximum population estimate). The task requires creating a GeoServer SLD style named 'cities_sorted' in the 'ne' workspace with a red (#FF0000) circle point symbolizer, configured with a sortBy VendorOption to sort cities by the pop_max attribute in ascending order (Painter's Algorithm: drawing smaller populations first ensures larger cities appear on top). The layer 'ne_populated_places' is published in GeoServer from a PostGIS database where Natural Earth shapefiles are imported with lowercased attribute names.
